_comment: Production config. NO secrets here - the SQL password comes from Key Vault (Sql:AdminPassword app setting) and the connection-string template from the Bicep (Sql:ConnectionStringTemplate). Local-dev values go in appsettings.Development.json or user-secrets, never committed.

Backstage._comment: Outbound speaker-bio sync (hub -> Zoho Backstage). INACTIVE by default and gated: a speaker is only ever made PUBLIC when SpeakerProfile.SelectedForPublish is true (defaults false). Real PortalId/EventId + OAuth creds are operator config (private config / Key Vault) - placeholders only here. Set Enabled=true only when a live writer is wired AND the lineup is selected.

Graphics._comment: SoMe-graphics SharePoint file store (REQUIREMENTS §18). INERT by default: with Enabled=false (or a blank SiteUrl) the hub still GENERATES graphics + builds share drafts, but stores nothing live and fakes no call (null file store). To enable the live store set Enabled=true and the per-edition SiteUrl/DriveName/RootFolderPath, AND configure the SPN under the 'SharePoint' section (TenantId/ClientId + CertificateThumbprint; cert on the host / Key Vault) which must hold a Sites.Selected grant on the site. SPN credentials are operator config - never commit values here.

SharePoint._sessionEvalsQrComment: REQUIREMENTS §124: drive-relative folder holding the per-room session-evaluation QR codes (files named with the room, e.g. Room-16-Floor-1-Device13.png). Speakers download the QR for their session's room; an org-admin uploads/replaces files here. Leave blank to keep the feature inert.

OpenAI._comment: The grounded AI Community Helper (code-named AiHelper; REQUIREMENTS §129). Azure OpenAI. INERT by default: with Enabled=false (or a blank Endpoint/Deployment/ApiKey) the assistant no-ops (widget hides, endpoint returns a friendly 'unavailable') and makes no call. ApiKey is a SECRET - NEVER commit it; set it as a Key Vault reference app setting in prod. Set Enabled=true and the per-resource Endpoint/Deployment only when a live deployment is wired. AssistantName is the operator-configurable user-facing display name (default 'Otto') shown in the widget + spoken in the system prompt; the code identifiers stay name-agnostic.

Feedback._comment: AiHelper INTAKE (REQUIREMENTS §137). The assistant detects bug/feature reports in a user's message (case-insensitive, whole-word keywords incl. Danish 'fejl'/'forslag') and forwards explicit 'contact the organizers' messages. Detected items are stored to the CEH feed (FeedbackItems) AND emailed: bug/feature -> BugFeatureEmailTo (dev mailbox), question -> OrganizerEmailTo (organizers). All values are config-overridable; defaults are the ELDK27 wiring. Set Enabled=false to turn off keyword intake (the explicit organizer path still works).

BecomeSponsor._comment: Public 'become a sponsor' CTA on /Sponsors (REQUIREMENTS §21). Not a secret (a sponsorship contact is meant to be public) but left BLANK here so a real address never lands in the public mirror - set per edition in private/operator config. With both ContactEmail and ContactUrl blank the CTA is hidden (no dead link). A non-blank ContactUrl (hosted prospectus/form) wins over ContactEmail (which builds a mailto). EmailSubjectFormat: {0} is the event name.
